_publications._publicationTitle: Impact of MEK Inhibitor Therapy on Neurocognitive Functioning in NF1.

_contextSnippet: At the 48-week follow-up, participants with impaired scores at pretreatment were significantly more likely to show meaningful improvement on MCI (71%) than those whose initial scores were not impaired (16%; χ2 = 9.45, p = 0.009, OR = 13.5)
details: At 48 weeks, MCI proportions were significantly different from normative population (5/70/25%; χ2 = 12.02, p = 0.002, OR = 6.3) with larger proportion classified as improved than expected. Participants with baseline MCI ratings in impaired range (≤1.5 SDs below mean, n=11; 19%) were more likely to show significant improvement - 71% improved at 48 weeks vs 16% of non-impaired (χ2 = 9.45, p = 0.009, OR = 13.5)
